meaning: n.专家(或权威等)的学问(或方法、见解)
examples: But predicting such events is more in the realms of astrology than financial punditry.
The other problem is this: contemporary punditry lives in the world of superficial tactics and interests.
Katie Couric will also host a special Webcast that night with reporters and punditry, for which viewers can submit questions.
Kinsley, one gathers from his long career in punditry, is a sort of left-leaning libertarian himself.
The easiest job in political punditry is to laugh off Mississippi Governor Haley Barbour.
